def test_style_case_insensitive():
        """Test that style names are case insensitive."""
        csv_data = "A,B\n1,2"
        result1 = run("-s", "MARKDOWN", stdin=csv_data)
        result2 = run("-s", "markdown", stdin=csv_data)
        result3 = run("-s", "MarkDown", stdin=csv_data)
>       assert result1.returncode == 0
E       assert 2 == 0
E        +  where 2 = CompletedProcess(args=['./executable', '-s', 'MARKDOWN'], returncode=2, stdout=b'', stderr=b"error: invalid value 'MARKDOWN' for '--style <STYLE>'\n  [possible values: none, ascii, ascii2, sharp, rounded, reinforced, markdown, grid]\n\nFor more information, try '--help'.\n").returncode

sample_csv = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-root/pytest-0/popen-gw1/test_flag_value_forms_are_equi2/sample.csv')

    def test_flag_value_forms_are_equivalent_for_success(sample_csv):
        # Ensure parsing accepts -dX, -d=X, --delimiter X, --delimiter=X.
        cases = [
            (["-d", ",", "-P", str(sample_csv)], 0),
            (["-d=,", "-P", str(sample_csv)], 0),
            (["--delimiter", ",", "-P", str(sample_csv)], 0),
            (["--delimiter=,", "-P", str(sample_csv)], 0),
        ]
        for args, expected_rc in cases:
            rc, out, err = run(args)
>           assert rc == expected_rc
E           assert 2 == 0
